hero.heading: A recovery-first project for keeping knowledge usable over time.
hero.summary: PUNNARAJ is being built as a documented system for preserving project context, decisions, and recovery paths across people, machines, and tools.

steps.title: Human authority
steps.detail: A person records knowledge and decisions.

steps.title: Reviewed source
steps.detail: Content is written, reviewed, and attributed.

steps.title: Verification
steps.detail: Claims, files, and links are checked.

steps.title: Publication
steps.detail: Reviewed projections are published with change history.

why.title: Context loss
why.body: Files can survive while decisions, assumptions, and reasons are forgotten or separated from them.

why.title: Changing platforms
why.body: Devices, services, file formats, and AI tools change. Durable source and documented recovery paths reduce dependence on one platform.

why.title: Public / private boundaries
why.body: Some knowledge can be public, some must remain private, and some should be shared selectively. The boundary must be explicit.

architecture.heading: One reviewed source, two public surfaces.
architecture.summary: Git is the record. Each publication is a controlled projection of the same reviewed source.
architecture.privateBoundary: Zero Vault + private evidence — never public
architecture.privateDetail: Excluded from public builds, repositories, and artifact storage.
architecture.databaseDecision: D1 is deferred until dynamic state is required.

evidence.heading: What is verified today
limits: No single image boots every hardware family
Orange Pi and M5Stack need exact models
Private family material is not public
Alpha URLs and schemas may change

disclaimer: This page reports current evidence and design decisions. It is not a claim of completion.
